edible_parts: Young pods (okra-like, cooked) and seeds (oily); black fiber for basketry.
other_uses: Sticky sprawling desert annual whose dry pods split into two vicious curved claws.
prep: Cook the tender young green pods like okra and eat the oil-rich seeds from mature pods; the strong black fiber peeled from the dry claws is a prized basketry material (cultivated by the Tohono O'odham for it).
risks: Mature claws catch skin and clothing.
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
